hyperedges.label: Shared Infrastructure Dependencies (ChromaDB + PostgreSQL + LLM API)

nodes.label: Structured logging configuration using structlog.  Console (human-readable) re

nodes.label: ChromaIndexer — embed chunks and run filtered vector search (spec §4 stage 5).
nodes.norm_label: chromaindexer — embed chunks and run filtered vector search (spec §4 stage 5).

nodes.label: Embeds document chunks and stores/queries them in a vector backend.
nodes.norm_label: embeds document chunks and stores/queries them in a vector backend.

nodes.label: Embed ``chunks`` and upsert them with filterable metadata.
nodes.norm_label: embed ``chunks`` and upsert them with filterable metadata.

nodes.label: Embed ``text`` and return the nearest chunks, optionally filtered.
nodes.norm_label: embed ``text`` and return the nearest chunks, optionally filtered.

nodes.label: Domain tuning constants.  These are fixed algorithm parameters, intentionally
nodes.norm_label: domain tuning constants.  these are fixed algorithm parameters, intentionally

nodes.label: DatabaseManager — relational persistence interface (spec §3.1, §4 stage 6).  D
nodes.norm_label: databasemanager — relational persistence interface (spec §3.1, §4 stage 6).  d

nodes.label: Everything persisted for one document (spec §8 documents/keywords).
nodes.norm_label: everything persisted for one document (spec §8 documents/keywords).

nodes.label: Atomic persistence of a document and its derived rows.
nodes.norm_label: atomic persistence of a document and its derived rows.

nodes.label: Return whether a document with this content hash already exists (dedup).
nodes.norm_label: return whether a document with this content hash already exists (dedup).

nodes.label: Persist document + keywords + chunk links atomically; rollback on error.
nodes.norm_label: persist document + keywords + chunk links atomically; rollback on error.

nodes.label: Non-durable implementation for tests — commits atomically into a dict.
nodes.norm_label: non-durable implementation for tests — commits atomically into a dict.

nodes.norm_label: documentanalyzer — infer document type and language (spec §4 stage 2).  type f

nodes.label: Classifies a document by length and dominant language.
nodes.norm_label: classifies a document by length and dominant language.

nodes.label: Return the inferred type and language for ``text``.
nodes.norm_label: return the inferred type and language for ``text``.

nodes.label: Cyrillic-dominant → ru, otherwise en (Latin is the default script).
nodes.norm_label: cyrillic-dominant → ru, otherwise en (latin is the default script).

nodes.label: DocumentSplitter — paragraph-aware chunking (spec §4 stage 4).  Packs whole pa
nodes.norm_label: documentsplitter — paragraph-aware chunking (spec §4 stage 4).  packs whole pa

nodes.label: Splits normalized text into overlapping, paragraph-aligned chunks.
nodes.norm_label: splits normalized text into overlapping, paragraph-aligned chunks.

nodes.label: Return ordered chunks covering ``text`` with overlap.
nodes.norm_label: return ordered chunks covering ``text`` with overlap.

nodes.label: Document class inferred from word count (DocumentAnalyzer).
nodes.norm_label: document class inferred from word count (documentanalyzer).

nodes.label: Dominant language detected by character composition (Cyrillic vs Latin).
nodes.norm_label: dominant language detected by character composition (cyrillic vs latin).

nodes.norm_label: metadataextractor — bibliographic metadata via llm (spec §4 stage 3).  feeds t

nodes.label: Extracts structured bibliographic metadata using an injected LLM.
nodes.norm_label: extracts structured bibliographic metadata using an injected llm.

nodes.label: Return metadata for ``text``; fall back to the filename on failure.
nodes.norm_label: return metadata for ``text``; fall back to the filename on failure.

nodes.label: Last resort: derive a human title from the file stem.
nodes.norm_label: last resort: derive a human title from the file stem.

nodes.label: Domain value objects that cross module boundaries (spec §3.1, §8).  Pydantic m
nodes.norm_label: domain value objects that cross module boundaries (spec §3.1, §8).  pydantic m

nodes.label: Bibliographic metadata extracted by the LLM (spec §3.1, §4 stage 3).
nodes.norm_label: bibliographic metadata extracted by the llm (spec §3.1, §4 stage 3).

nodes.norm_label: a text fragment produced by documentsplitter (spec §4 stage 4).

nodes.label: A retrieval result from the vector store (spec §5 step 3).
nodes.norm_label: a retrieval result from the vector store (spec §5 step 3).

nodes.norm_label: ragpipeline — orchestrates the domain modules (spec §3.1, §4, §5).  two modes:

nodes.label: Coordinates extraction, analysis, indexing and persistence.

nodes.label: Run the six indexing stages and return a summary (spec §4).
nodes.norm_label: run the six indexing stages and return a summary (spec §4).

nodes.label: Retrieve the chunks most relevant to ``query`` (filtered, ranked).          TO
nodes.norm_label: retrieve the chunks most relevant to ``query`` (filtered, ranked).          to

nodes.norm_label: injected-dependency interfaces for the domain layer.  the domain modules never

nodes.norm_label: minimal async text-completion interface (real impl: llm/ factory, phase 3).

nodes.label: Return one dense vector per input text.

nodes.norm_label: extracts normalized text from a document file.

nodes.norm_label: read ``path`` and return extracted text with quality metadata.

nodes.norm_label: return (text, n_pages); n_pages is 0 when the format has no page concept.

nodes.norm_label: trim, unify newlines, and collapse excessive blank lines.

nodes.label: Base class for all domain-layer errors.
nodes.norm_label: base class for all domain-layer errors.

nodes.label: Raised when a file extension is outside the supported allowlist.
nodes.norm_label: raised when a file extension is outside the supported allowlist.

nodes.label: Raised when the LLM fails to yield valid metadata after all retries.
nodes.norm_label: raised when the llm fails to yield valid metadata after all retries.

nodes.norm_label: raised when a document with the same content hash is already indexed (§8 dedup).
